details: The scheme "Financial Assistance for Medical" is a scheme by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir. The scheme provides financial aid to registered workers' children for various medical needs, provided the worker has been engaged in building or construction work for at least 90 days in the preceding 12 months. Under this scheme, workers can receive ₹5,000/- for injury treatment, ₹10,000/- for temporary disability, ₹75,000/- for permanent disability, and up to ₹1,00,000/- for the treatment of chronic diseases.
benefits: Financial Assistance in the Case of Injury: ₹5,000/-
Financial Assistance in the Case of Temporary Disability: ₹10,000/-
Financial Assistance in the Case of Permanent Disability: ₹75,000/-
Financial Assistancein the Case of Treatment of Chronic Diseases: ₹1,00,000/-
eligibility: For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker -
The applicant should be a resident of Jammu & Kashmir.
The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.
The applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
The applicant should have engaged in any building or other construction work for at least 90 days during the preceding 12 months.
The applicant should be registration is renewed annually.
The applicant must be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Jammu & Kashmir.
The applicant should be suffering from an injury, a disability, or from a chronic disease.

online: Go to the J&K Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (JKBOCWWB)
On the homepage, find and click the "
If you're a new user, click on "Apply for New Registration."
Complete the registration form with your details, submit the form, and send the OTP to your registered mobile number. Enter the OTP and click "Verify OTP & Submit."
After the OTP is verified, a confirmation message with your Login ID and password will be sent to your registered mobile number.
Use the Login ID, password, and captcha to log in (remember, the username and password are case-sensitive). filled are case-sensitive). Read all the instructions carefully and Click to continue
Once logged in, choose the "Construction Worker’s Registration" option and click on "Registration Form."
A dashboard will appear. Complete the form by entering all required details (marked with *).
Upload all the important documents, such as ID proof and other relevant certificates.
Once the form is completed and documents uploaded, click "Submit" and pay the application fee online. You will receive a registration number after successful payment and submission.

documents_required: Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
Certificate of Medical Officer of Government or Private Empanelled hospital
Original Bill of expenditure on the treatment

faqs.question: What is the Financial Assistance for Medical scheme?
faqs.answer: It provides financial aid for registered workers' medical expenses for injuries, disabilities, and chronic diseases.

faqs.question: Who can apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Registered building or construction workers in Jammu & Kashmir.

faqs.question: What is the minimum duration of work required to register?
faqs.answer: Workers must have been employed in building or construction for at least 90 days in the past year.

faqs.question: How much assistance is provided for injury treatment?
faqs.answer: The scheme offers ₹5,000 for injury treatment.

faqs.question: What financial support is available for temporary disability?
What is the amount provided for permanent disability?
faqs.answer: Financial aid of ₹75,000 is available for permanent disability.

faqs.question: What assistance is given for chronic disease treatment?
faqs.answer: Up to ₹1,00,000 is provided for chronic disease treatment.

faqs.question: Is there an age limit for registration?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants should be between 18 and 60 years.

faqs.question: Do I need to be a resident of Jammu & Kashmir to apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, only residents of Jammu & Kashmir are eligible.

faqs.question: Can my children benefit from this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme also covers medical aid for registered workers' children.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply?
faqs.answer: Proof of registration, medical records, and relevant ID are typically required.
